upset_picks.reasoning: Akron won 29 games including final 10, MAC Tournament Champions, 7.5-point underdogs, +260 ML. Texas Tech dealing with multiple injuries to star players.
Cleanest single-game upset on the bracket. UNC opened only -2.5. VCU: 81.6 PPG, 54.1% eFG, 31.3% offensive-rebound rate, 36.7% from three, 13.0% turnover rate.
24-8, AAC Tournament champions, only 5.5-point underdogs (+185 ML), 88.3 PPG (8th nationally). Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. has missed four games with back injury.

dark_horse_final_four_picks.reasoning: 19th offensive, 25th defensive efficiency — one of only 8 teams top-25 in both. 59.7% two-point shooting (9th). Duke's injuries create vulnerability. Weakness: 173rd in forcing turnovers.
7th offensive, 31st defensive efficiency. Strong ball security (13.3% TO rate, 11th). Elite FT shooting (79.3%, 4th). Four seniors in starting lineup. Could upset Florida twice in one season.

27th offensive, 18th defensive efficiency. Strong perimeter defense. Two reliable three-point shooters. Nine players average 16+ minutes. Michigan's Cason injury and Texas Tech's Toppin injury create favorable path.
Elite turnover management (12.8% TO rate, 3rd). 78.6% FT (12th). 42.5% of shots from three (4th nationally). Veteran guards Nick Boyd and John Blackwell. Beaten Illinois, Purdue, MSU, Michigan.

upset_picks.reasoning: High Point can score, but Wisconsin's guards Boyd and Blackwell provide tough matchup
Without Caleb Wilson, UNC has a lower ceiling. VCU is a fair upset pick.
Louisville's Mikel Brown Jr. sidelined with sore back. Both teams shoot many threes — creates rebounding chaos.
Kentucky banged up and inconsistent. Santa Clara has positional size at almost every position.
UCLA banged up. UCF's Jamichael Stillwell resembles Draymond Green defensively.

consensus_summary.note: Post-Selection Sunday analysis — actual bracket is now known
consensus_summary.champion_reasoning: Arizona has the combination of elite backcourt (Jaden Bradley, Brayden Burries), size/physicality, no significant injuries, and the weakest regional path (West). 8 of 19 experts pick them to cut down the nets for the first time since 1997.

West.upset_alert: High Point over Wisconsin, Utah State over Villanova

Midwest.upset_alert: Akron over Texas Tech, Santa Clara over Kentucky

Milan Momcilovic is the best 3-point shooter in the field. 4th-best defense nationally. Fits PoolGenius champion formula.
7th offensive efficiency, 31st defensive. Four-senior starting lineup. +1100 dark horse odds. Best free throw shooting team (79.3%, 4th).

Criminally underseeded at No. 5. Big East POY Zuby Ejiofor. Rick Pitino coaching. Won 19 of last 20 games.

trending_upsets.matchup: No. 12 High Point over No. 5 Wisconsin
trending_upsets.reason: High Point plays full-court pressure defense. Wisconsin's offensive style could be disrupted.

key_narratives: Arizona is the consensus champion pick (8 of 19 experts) despite being the 3rd-best odds (+360-400). The market may be undervaluing them.
Duke has the toughest path of any 1-seed — faces UConn and Michigan State before the Final Four — yet still gets 6 champion picks.
Michigan's L.J. Cason ACL injury has cratered expert confidence — only 2 champion picks despite +340 odds.
The South Region is a gauntlet: defending champion Florida, Houston (2), Illinois (3), Vanderbilt (5) — no easy path.
Tom Izzo's Michigan State is the consensus East Region dark horse — Bilas and Greenberg both pick MSU over Duke.
Injury context is driving more upset picks than usual — Louisville, UNC, Texas Tech, Michigan, and Duke all dealing with significant absences.
BYU's AJ Dybantsa is called 'the best player in the tournament' by The Ringer — a 6-seed with the projected No. 1 NBA draft pick.
Florida's three-point shooting improvement (23.4% to 32.6%) is either a real transformation or a mirage that could doom their title defense.
consensus_summary.consensus_narrative: Arizona has emerged as the post-bracket-reveal consensus champion pick, overtaking pre-Selection Sunday favorite Duke. The Wildcats benefit from no significant injuries, the weakest regional path (West), elite guard play (Bradley/Burries), and physical size. Duke's injury concerns (Foster out, Ngongba questionable) and brutal East Region draw have dampened enthusiasm despite having the best player (Cameron Boozer) and best efficiency margin. Michigan's Cason ACL injury is the tournament's biggest wildcard. The South Region is universally regarded as the toughest, with defending champion Florida likely facing Houston and/or Illinois. Injury-driven upsets (South Florida over Louisville, VCU over UNC, Akron over Texas Tech) are this year's most popular contrarian picks.

methodology_notes.limitation: Selection Sunday was March 15, 2026. This file reflects post-bracket-reveal expert analysis from March 16, 2026. Tournament games begin March 18 (First Four) and March 20 (first round). Some model-based predictions (CBS SportsLine, full Nate Silver probabilities) are behind paywalls.
methodology_notes.change_from_march_12: Major shift: Arizona replaced Duke as consensus champion pick after bracket reveal showed Arizona has easiest path. Michigan dropped significantly due to L.J. Cason ACL injury confirmed after March 12. Duke's injuries (Foster, Ngongba) became clearer. Actual seedings now known vs. projections.
